title: Pungent Syrian Challah Bake
description: A kid-friendly, cheese-pull Syrian dish featuring challah bread, perfect for a family dinner. This keto-friendly recipe is a fun twist on traditional bread pudding.

ingredients.name: 4 cups challah bread, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 pound beef roast, cooked and shredded

instructions: In a large skillet, cook the diced onions and minced garlic over medium heat until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the shredded beef, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs, heavy cream, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Add the cubed challah bread to the egg mixture and toss until well coated.
Pour the bread mixture into a greased 9x13-inch baking dish and top with the cooked onion and beef mixture, followed by the grated cheddar and mozzarella cheese.
Dot the top of the cheese with the unsalted butter.
Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
Let rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Slice and serve hot, enjoying the gooey cheese pull!
Serve warm, garnished with additional chopped parsley if desired.
This dish is best served immediately, as the cheese will start to set after about 10-15 minutes.
If you prefer a crisper top, broil for an additional 2-3 minutes, watching closely to avoid burning.
